info.name: Clotilde - Duffel API Testing
info.description: API test suite for the Duffel flight search integration used in Clotilde Travel Bot (duffel.js). Covers happy path, policy compliance, and negative/edge cases.

item.name: 1 - Happy Path
item.description: Core flow: mirrors exactly what duffel.js sends for a valid Economy search

item.name: 5 - Response Schema Validation
item.description: Verify every field that duffel.js mapOffer() reads actually exists in the API response. If any field is missing, the bot silently returns wrong data.

item.name: RS-01 | All mapOffer() fields present (flight_number, airline, datetime, stops)

item.name: RS-03 | Top-3 cap — API returns more than 3, duffel.js must cap it

item.name: 6 - Preferred Airline & Long-Haul Coverage
item.description: Policy.js has 3 preferred airlines (TG, SQ, EK). Tests for SQ and EK were missing. Also covers long-haul duration classification (>4h = LONG_HAUL cap 25000 THB).

item.name: PA-01 | SQ (Singapore Airlines) — preferred partner for SIN routes

exec: pm.test('iata_code field exists on all carriers (needed for SQ detection)', () => {
pm.test('SQ (Singapore Airlines) in results — policy preferred partner for SIN', () => {
if (!hasSQ) console.warn('SQ not in results today — verify manually');

item.name: PA-02 | EK (Emirates) — preferred partner for Middle East & Europe routes

exec: pm.test('iata_code field exists (needed for EK detection)', () => {

item.name: PA-04 | ZZ airline (Duffel sandbox) must NOT appear in results

exec: // duffel.js explicitly filters offers where iata_code === 'ZZ'
// (Duffel Airways = sandbox test airline, not a real carrier)

console.warn('ZZ found in raw API response — duffel.js filter is needed and working');
console.log('ZZ not present in this response');
pm.expect(true).to.be.true; // document only — filter is in duffel.js, not API
